clusters.title: Former Federal Reserve Chairman Alan Greenspan Dies at 100
clusters.summary: Alan Greenspan, who served as the Chairman of the Federal Reserve for 18 years under four U.S. presidents, has passed away at the age of 100. His tenure was marked by significant economic events, including both crises and periods of growth, influencing monetary policy in the United States. Greenspan's legacy continues to shape discussions on economic management and regulation.

clusters.title: Music Mogul Clive Davis Passes Away at 94
clusters.summary: Clive Davis, a prominent figure in the music industry known for his role in launching and nurturing the careers of artists such as Whitney Houston and Bruce Springsteen, has died at the age of 94. His influence as the founder of Arista Records and former president of Columbia Records significantly shaped the landscape of modern music. Davis's legacy includes a remarkable ability to identify and promote talent across multiple genres.

items.title: Clive Davis, Music Industry Titan Who Signed Whitney Houston, Dies at 94
items.description: He rose from a midlevel position at Columbia Records to become one of music’s most powerful executives, shepherding stars like Barry Manilow and Whitney Houston.

items.title: Clive Davis, music mogul behind Whitney Houston and Bruce Springsteen, dies aged 94
items.description: The record company executive also worked with Billy Joel, Pink Floyd, Alicia Keys and Aerosmith.

items.title: Clive Davis, music icon who signed Whitney Houston and Carrie Underwood, dead at 94
items.description: Legendary record label exec Clive Davis has died. He helped launch careers of Whitney Houston, Bruce Springsteen, Alicia Keys and Carlos Santana.

items.title: Clive Davis, mogul who nurtured musicians from Janis Joplin to Whitney Houston, dies at 94 - Los Angeles Times
items.description: Clive Davis, mogul who nurtured musicians from Janis Joplin to Whitney Houston, dies at 94  Los Angeles Times

items.title: Clive Davis: music industry executive who signed Whitney Houston and Bruce Springsteen dies aged 94
items.description:
Davis, who discovered many of the defining musicians of the 20th century and helmed major record labels, said he ‘never’ tired of the music business

The famed US music industry executive and record producer Clive Davis has died aged 94, his family has confirmed.

He had recently been hospitalised with respiratory problems and was recovering at home. He had also been diagnosed with neurological condition Bell’s palsy in 2021.

Continue reading...

items.description: Entertainment icon Clive Davis, who shaped modern music as the founder of Arista Records and president of Columbia Records, died Monday, family members told NBC News

clusters.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Resigns Amid Party Rebellion
clusters.summary: Keir Starmer has announced his resignation as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom following a rebellion from members within his own party. Starmer, who took office in 2024 after a significant electoral victory, is stepping down amid growing dissent, which has raised concerns about the stability of the current government. His resignation marks a pivotal moment in British politics, prompting discussions about potential leadership changes and the future direction of the Labour Party.

items.title: Keir Starmer Resigns as UK Prime Minister
items.description: His departure in the coming weeks clears a path for Andy Burnham, a popular Labour Party mayor, to become the country’s seventh prime minister in a decade.

clusters.title: U.S. and Iran Engage in High-Level Talks in Switzerland
clusters.summary: U.S. officials, led by Vance, are engaged in high-level discussions with Iranian representatives in Switzerland aimed at establishing an interim deal to end ongoing conflicts. While Vance described the initial talks as laying a 'good foundation,' tensions remain as former President Trump issued threats towards Tehran. The outcome of these negotiations could significantly impact U.S.-Iran relations and regional stability.

clusters.title: Supreme Court Reinstates Murder Conviction in Etan Patz Case
clusters.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court has reinstated the murder conviction of Pedro Hernandez, who was found guilty in 2017 for the kidnapping and murder of six-year-old Etan Patz, who disappeared in 1979. This decision overturns a previous appeals court ruling that had granted Hernandez a new trial, reigniting attention on a case that has haunted New York City for decades. The ruling underscores the complexities of long-standing legal battles in high-profile missing persons cases.

items.title: Supreme Court reinstates murder conviction in case of Etan Patz, missing New York City boy - AP News
items.description: Supreme Court reinstates murder conviction in case of Etan Patz, missing New York City boy  AP News

items.title: US supreme court reinstates murder conviction in case of Etan Patz
items.description:
Appeals court had ruled Pedro Hernandez, 64, was wrongly convicted over 1979 disappearance of New York six-year-old

The US supreme court has reinstated a murder conviction in the long winding case of Etan Patz, whose 1979 disappearance at age six from New York City garnered national headlines.

In a 6-3 decision on Monday, the supreme court agreed with New York prosecutors in their request to reverse a lower court ruling that had thrown out the murder conviction of Pedro Hernandez, 64, in the Patz case.

Continue reading...

items.title: Supreme Court Restores Conviction in 1979 Murder of Etan Patz
items.description: Pedro Hernandez was convicted in 2017 of kidnapping and murdering the 6-year-old boy, but an appeals court in July had said Mr. Hernandez was entitled to a new trial.

clusters.title: Three Hikers Die as Extreme Heat Hits Grand Canyon National Park
clusters.summary: Three hikers have died in Grand Canyon National Park due to extreme heat conditions, prompting officials to issue warnings for visitors. The National Park Service is advising caution as temperatures are expected to rise again early next week, highlighting the dangers of hiking in such severe weather. The incidents underscore the importance of preparedness and awareness of environmental conditions when visiting the park.

clusters.summary: A massive fire at a luxury resort in the Dominican Republic resulted in one death and nine injuries, prompting the evacuation of nearly 1,700 tourists. The blaze, which destroyed significant portions of the facility, raised concerns about safety protocols in tourist areas. Local authorities are investigating the cause of the fire while ensuring the safety and well-being of those affected.

clusters.title: Ubisoft Co-Founder Claude Guillemot Dies in Plane Crash
clusters.summary: Claude Guillemot, co-founder of the video game company Ubisoft, was killed in a plane crash in western France at the age of 69. His contributions to the gaming industry, including the creation of the popular 'Assassin’s Creed' franchise, have left a lasting impact. The incident has prompted an outpouring of tributes from the gaming community and beyond, highlighting his legacy in the industry.

clusters.summary: Marketa Vondrousova, the 2023 Wimbledon champion, has been handed a four-year suspension after she refused to take an anti-doping test in December 2025. This decision underscores the strict enforcement of doping regulations in professional sports. The ban raises questions about athlete compliance with testing protocols and the implications for Vondrousova's career.

clusters.title: Keir Starmer Resigns as Labour Leader, Successor Search Begins
clusters.summary: Keir Starmer has announced his resignation as leader of the Labour Party, stating he will remain in the role until a successor is appointed. The move has sparked discussions about potential candidates, including Andy Burnham, who is seen as a unifying figure capable of reconnecting the party with its voter base. This leadership change comes at a critical time for Labour as it seeks to strengthen its position ahead of upcoming elections.

clusters.summary: Meta has announced a $900 million investment in the Indian fintech startup CRED, which rewards users for paying their credit card bills. As part of this strategic move, Meta has appointed CRED's founder to lead its WhatsApp operations, aiming to enhance the platform's growth and integration in the Indian market. This investment reflects Meta's commitment to expanding its presence in India’s rapidly growing digital economy.

clusters.title: Blast at Qatar LNG Facility Kills 13, Injures Dozens
clusters.summary: A blast at the Ras Laffan liquefied natural gas processing site in Qatar resulted in at least 13 fatalities and left 66 others injured. The explosion occurred during a restart operation at the facility, with reports indicating that 18 individuals are still unaccounted for. This incident highlights safety concerns in the energy sector as investigations into the cause are underway.

clusters.summary: The U.S. military conducted a strike on a boat suspected of drug trafficking in the eastern Pacific Ocean, resulting in the deaths of two individuals and leaving six survivors. This operation is part of an ongoing campaign targeting drug smuggling activities in Latin America. The U.S. Coast Guard has been notified about the survivors, but further details regarding their rescue have not been disclosed.

clusters.summary: U.S. Senator Marco Rubio is traveling to the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait, and Bahrain from June 23 to June 25 to engage in discussions regarding the recent memorandum of understanding with Iran and security concerns in the Strait of Hormuz. The visit aims to strengthen ties with Gulf allies amid ongoing regional tensions and to address shared priorities related to Iran's influence. This diplomatic effort underscores the importance of collaboration between the U.S. and its partners in the Middle East.
